mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% YoY increase in industrial production over the past 6 months signals a strengthening demand environment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a tightening labor market, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% YoY increase in housing starts over the past 6 months signals a strengthening supply environment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening supply periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 10% YoY increase in real disposable personal income over the past 6 months signals a strengthening demand environment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in payroll employment above 100k signals a strengthening labor market, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in strengthening labor market periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the Consumer Price Index above 2.5% signals an inflationary monetary environment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in inflationary monetary periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production and a 10% YoY increase in real disposable personal income signals a strengthening demand environment, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening demand periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
